summarization.a: The line chart illustrates the mass changes over time at 300°C on Al₂O₃ during the sequential dosing of TMA and HF,  showing a stepwise decrease in mass.

a.question: What describes the figure presented?
a.answer: The figure shows a zoom-in view of the entire mass change graph. Here, it is possible to observe the mass change after TMA and HF dosing at 300 °C on Al2O3, with decreasing values over the time and slight mass gains after the HF dose.

a.question: How does mass change over the time?
a.answer: The figure shows a slight decrease as time increases. After the TMA dose, a mass loss can be observed. Subsequently, the HF dose leads to mass gain.

a.question: Which reactant causes a loss of mass over time?
Why does TMA produce a loss of mass over time?
a.answer: TMA can etch the fluorinated Al2O3 films, removing the AlF3 from the surface. This explains why decreases are observed after TMA dose.
